name: Balor Extended
description: Balor, also known as Balor of the Evil Eye, was a formidable figure in Irish mythology, a king of the Fomorians, a race of supernatural beings often depicted as enemies of the Tuatha Dé Danann. He is primarily known for his single, immensely powerful eye that could instantly kill or destroy anything it gazed upon. This eye, requiring four men to lift its eyelid, was a weapon of unparalleled destruction. Balor's origins are shrouded in mystery, with some accounts suggesting he was born with the eye, while others claim it gained its power through exposure to a poisonous concoction or a magical ritual. He was the father of Ethniu, who was prophesied to give birth to his downfall, Lugh. Driven by this prophecy, Balor imprisoned Ethniu, but she eventually escaped and bore Lugh. Balor's role in Irish mythology is primarily as a powerful antagonist, a representation of the destructive forces of nature or the chaotic power of the old gods. His defeat at the hands of Lugh during the Second Battle of Mag Tuired is a pivotal moment, symbolizing the triumph of the Tuatha Dé Danann and the ushering in of a new era. He represents raw power, destruction, and the fear of the unknown. His legacy continues to resonate in Irish folklore, a reminder of the potent and sometimes terrifying forces that shaped the ancient world. Balor's influence extends beyond his destructive eye; he embodies the fearsome power of the Fomorians and the constant struggle between order and chaos in Irish mythology. His image is one of immense size and strength, a king who commanded fear and respect among his own people and terror among his enemies. The tales of Balor serve as cautionary narratives about the dangers of unchecked power and the inevitability of fate.
subtitle: Balor of the Evil Eye

keyMyths.title: Balor's Eye
keyMyths.description: Balor's eye was so powerful that it required four men to lift its eyelid. When opened, it could kill anything it gazed upon, making him a fearsome warrior.
keyMyths.source: The Second Battle of Mag Tuired

keyMyths.title: The Prophecy of Lugh
keyMyths.description: A prophecy foretold that Balor would be killed by his grandson. To prevent this, he imprisoned his daughter Ethniu, but she eventually gave birth to Lugh.

keyMyths.title: Balor's Death
keyMyths.description: During the Second Battle of Mag Tuired, Lugh defeated Balor by striking his evil eye with a sling stone, killing him and securing victory for the Tuatha Dé Danann.
keyMyths.source: The Second Battle of Mag Tuired
